title: [lichen_link_rx.c:229-236] tc_sha256 return values silently ignored
description:
Gap: lichen_link_rx.c uses tc_sha256 functions but ignores their return values, unlike the centralized lichen_sha256 in lichen_util.c.

What: Lines 229-236 call tc_sha256_init, tc_sha256_update, and tc_sha256_final with (void) casts, ignoring any errors. While TinyCrypt's SHA-256 functions typically only fail on NULL pointers (which cannot happen here), the inconsistency with lichen_sha256() creates two different error handling patterns.

Why it matters: If TinyCrypt behavior changes (e.g., internal state corruption detection), errors would be silently swallowed. The EUI-64 derivation would produce garbage hash data used for peer identification, potentially allowing frames to appear as from a different peer.
close_reason: Not external - this is project code in lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_rx.c. The tc_sha256 returns (lines 250-253) are explicitly cast to (void), a deliberate idiom indicating intentional ignore. Failure is impossible: sha_state is a valid stack variable, ctx->peer_pubkey is guarded by has_peer_identity check, and hash is a valid stack array. The (void) casts satisfy static analyzers while documenting the intent.
